page: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 architecture server powered by an AMD EPYC 7542 processor. It features 16 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with a single thread per core, operating at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is equipped with 48.0 GB of system memory, providing 3.0 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and runs on the KVM hypervisor with shared CPU allocation.

Benchmark data indicates a clear performance tradeoff. The shared CPU allocation results in poor single-core CPU performance, placing in the bottom 10% of stress-ng single-core benchmarks. However, multi-threaded capabilities are more robust, with bzip3 multi-threaded decompression scoring in the top 25% at 149.23 GB/sec. Most other metrics, including Geekbench multi-core, Passmark CPU, and Redis SET operations, fall within the average range.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the shared CPU model offers a high density of vCPUs and memory resources at a lower cost profile, making it suitable for workloads that can tolerate shared CPU scheduling. Appropriate workloads include multi-threaded data processing, static web serving, and moderate database operations, whereas tasks requiring sustained, high-performance single-core execution are less suitable.
description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 server featuring 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 48.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show a distinct tradeoff: single-core CPU performance is poor, ranking in the bottom 10% on stress-ng, while multi-threaded operations like bzip3 decompression perform strongly in the top 25%. Most other benchmarks, including memory bandwidth, Redis SET operations, and Geekbench scores, fall in the average tier. Qualitatively, the shared CPU allocation provides a cost-effective resource density for multi-threaded workloads. This server is suited for static web serving, multi-threaded data processing, and moderate database operations that do not require dedicated single-core CPU performance.
og_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 48 GB RAM. It offers average multi-core performance but limited single-core capacity, making it a budget-friendly multi-threaded option.
meta_description: UpCloud CLOUDNATIVE-16xCPU-48GB is an x86_64 server with 16 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs and 48 GB RAM, suited for budget multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A shared-CPU instance offering sixteen cores and forty-eight gigabytes of memory for cost-effective, multi-threaded data processing workloads.
bullet_points: 16 shared AMD EPYC 7542 vCPUs and 48.0 GB of system memory
KVM hypervisor architecture with x86_64 compatibility
Strong multi-threaded bzip3 decompression performance in the top 25%
Poor single-core CPU performance due to shared resource allocation
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware included
Best suited for static web serving and multi-threaded batch processing
